# Phase 27 Plan 03: Real-Device iOS Standalone-Mode UAT

## Build Under Test

**Bundle:** Production build in `dist/` (generated by `tsc -b && vite build` as part of Plan 27-03 Task 2).

**App version:** v1.3 (Phase 27 — PWA Install & Offline)

**How to serve for device UAT:**

> The service worker requires HTTPS (or `localhost`) to register — plain `http://` on a remote host will NOT register the SW, and offline mode will not work.

1. **Recommended (production host):** Deploy `dist/` to the GitHub Pages host at `https://<owner>.github.io/hrv/`. The app is already live there; redeploy the new `dist/` output and test from that URL.

2. **Alternative (localhost):** Run `npm run preview` — Vite serves the `dist/` build on `http://localhost:4173/hrv/`. Service workers register on `localhost` even without TLS, so offline testing works. To test from a physical iOS device on the same LAN, serve over HTTPS (e.g. `vite preview --host --https` with a self-signed cert, or use a tool such as `mkcert`).

3. **HTTPS tunnel (fallback):** Tools such as `ngrok http 4173` expose the preview server over HTTPS on a temporary public URL.

---

## Test Device

| Field | Value |
|-------|-------|
| Device model | _(fill in — e.g. iPhone 15 Pro)_ |
| iOS version | _(fill in — e.g. iOS 18.3.2)_ |
| Safari version | _(fill in — e.g. Safari 17.x)_ |
| Test date | _(fill in)_ |
| Tester | _(fill in)_ |

> **Note for iOS-3:** Wake Lock behavior depends on the iOS version above. On iOS **>= 18.4** the screen should stay on throughout the session. On iOS **< 18.4** the screen may dim mid-session — this is the documented WebKit bug 254545 behavior and counts as a **PASS** (expected, not a regression).

---

## UAT Results Table

| # | Scenario | Pass Criteria | Result | Notes |
|---|----------|---------------|--------|-------|
| iOS-1 | Install via Safari Share → Add to Home Screen | App icon appears correctly; opens in standalone mode (no browser chrome) | | |
| iOS-2 | Launch from Home Screen, disable network, start session | Session runs; audio cues play; orb animates — fully offline | | |
| iOS-3 | Run a 2-min session, then put device to sleep | Session resumes correctly on wake if iOS ≥ 18.4; screen dims on iOS < 18.4 (documented behavior — PASS) | | |
| iOS-4 | Check app icon on Home Screen | Correct orb icon, not a screenshot thumbnail | | |
| iOS-5 | Check status bar in standalone mode | Theme color visible; no browser address bar | | |
| iOS-6 | Verify app name on Home Screen | Shows "HRV Breathing" | | |

**Result values:** `PASS` / `FAIL` / `SKIP` (with reason)

---

## Completion Sign-Off

Once all six scenarios have results recorded, the operator signals completion with one of:

- `"approved"` — all six scenarios recorded (any combination of PASS/FAIL/SKIP with documented reasons)
- A description of any failures found — the continuation agent will triage and decide next steps

If a real iOS device is genuinely unavailable, record that explicitly in the Notes column and describe the closest substitute attempted (e.g. iOS Simulator). Do not silently skip — the operator decides whether to accept a partial UAT at phase close.
